items.excerpt: Google unveiled Nano Banana 2 Lite (Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Image), which generates images in about 4 seconds at $0.034 per 1,000 images. It is positioned not as an artistic engine but as a high-throughput utility layer for automated design, programmatic ad, and digital-commerce workflows.

Google's Gemini Omni Flash is rolling out to developers and enterprises through an API, letting creative teams generate and then edit a finished video clip through conversation. It reframes video production as an iterative, natural-language dialogue rather than a manual timeline workflow.

Autodesk CMO Dara Treseder argues that the strongest creative and marketing teams will know when NOT to reach for AI, talking brand storytelling and keeping human judgment central as generative tools spread across design and creative work.
